sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa dell'aumento delle tensioni geopolitiche in Medio Oriente e dell'impatto sulla produzione di petrolio. Il VIX è sopra 20, il DXY è forte e il TLT è in calo. Il prezzo del petrolio è salito sopra $100/barile.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is risk-off due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and the impact on oil production. VIX is above 20, DXY is strong, and TLT is declining. Oil prices have surged above $100/barrel.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è in calo, con S&P 500 -0.61% e STOXX 600 -0.55%. Il prezzo del petrolio è salito del 4.10% a $98.91/barile. Il DXY è salito dello 0.13% a 100.50. Il VIX è sceso dello 0.37% a 27.19.
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market is down, with S&P 500 -0.61% and STOXX 600 -0.55%. Oil prices rose 4.10% to $98.91/barrel. DXY increased 0.13% to 100.50. VIX decreased 0.37% to 27.19.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno e benvenuti a questo aggiornamento finanziario. Oggi, 14 marzo 2026, il sentiment di mercato è decisamente risk-off, con un indice di paura VIX superiore a 20 e un Dollar Index DXY che si mantiene forte a 100,50. Il prezzo del petrolio è salito sopra i 100 dollari al barile, precisamente a 98,91 dollari per il Brent, con una variazione del -4,10% rispetto alla giornata precedente.

In questo contesto, analizziamo i dati chiave della giornata. L'EUR/USD si quota a 1,14, con una perdita del 0,85%, mentre l'S&P 500 mostra una discesa dello 0,61% a 6632,19. Il TLT Bond USA 20Y scende dello 0,49% a 86,54, e il rendimento del decennale USA si attesta al 4,28%, in aumento dello 0,28%. L'oro guadagna lo 0,18% a 5061,70 dollari l'oncia, mentre il Bitcoin si mantiene stabile a 70.691 dollari, con una lieve flessione dello 0,39%.

Passiamo ora all'analisi dei mercati. L'aumento delle tensioni geopolitiche in Medio Oriente sta avendo un impatto significativo sui prezzi del petrolio e, conseguentemente, sull'intero scenario economico globale. La situazione in Ucraina e le recenti dichiarazioni di leader mondiali stanno mantenendo alta la tensione, influenzando negativamente la propensione al rischio degli investitori.

Il Dollar Index DXY, che misura la forza del dollaro USA rispetto a un paniere di valute, sale dello 0,13% a 100,50, riflettendo la sua tradizionale funzione di valuta rifugio in momenti di incertezza. Contestualmente, l'EUR/USD mostra una flessione del 0,85% a 1,14, evidenziando la debolezza dell'euro rispetto al dollaro in questo contesto.

L'S&P 500, indice benchmark delle azioni statunitensi, perde lo 0,61% a 6632,19, mentre il VIX, indice della volatilità, scende leggermente dello 0,37% a 27,19, pur rimanendo su livelli elevati. Il mercato azionario statunitense è influenzato dalle preoccupazioni legate all'inflazione e alle mosse future delle banche centrali, in particolare la Federal Reserve.

La curva dei rendimenti dei Treasury USA mostra un aumento del rendimento del decennale dello 0,28% al 4,28%, mentre il TLT Bond USA 20Y scende dello 0,49% a 86,54. Questo movimento riflette le aspettative degli investitori riguardo alla futura politica monetaria della Fed e all'evoluzione dell'inflazione.

Sul fronte delle materie prime, l'oro, tradizionale asset rifugio, guadagna lo 0,18% a 5061,70 dollari l'oncia, mentre il prezzo del petrolio, dopo aver superato i 100 dollari al barile, si stabilizza a 98,91 dollari per il Brent, con una variazione del -4,10%.

Passiamo ora alla sezione geopolitica. Le tensioni in Medio Oriente continuano a dominare la scena, con un impatto diretto sui prezzi del petrolio e sull'andamento dei mercati finanziari. La situazione è tesa e gli investitori monitorano con attenzione ogni sviluppo, temendo un'ulteriore escalation che potrebbe avere conseguenze economiche significative.

La crisi in Medio Oriente non solo influenza i prezzi del petrolio ma anche la politica monetaria delle banche centrali. La Federal Reserve e la Banca Centrale Europea (BCE) stanno seguendo con attenzione la situazione, considerando le possibili implicazioni per l'inflazione e la crescita economica.

In merito ai dati macroeconomici, gli ultimi dati disponibili, risalenti al 1° febbraio 2026, mostrano un CPI YoY USA al 2,7%, un Core CPI YoY USA anch'esso al 2,7%, mentre il tasso di disoccupazione si attesta al 4,4%. Questi dati, sebbene non recentissimi, forniscono indicazioni utili sullo stato attuale dell'economia statunitense.

La Fed, in particolare, è attesa mantenere una politica monetaria restrittiva nel breve termine, con il Fed Funds Rate che si mantiene stabile al 3,64%. La prossima riunione della Fed sarà cruciale per determinare le mosse future in risposta all'evoluzione dell'inflazione e delle condizioni economiche.

Infine, guardando al futuro, gli investitori terranno d'occhio l'andamento delle tensioni geopolitiche, l'evoluzione dell'inflazione e le decisioni delle banche centrali. La pubblicazione dei dati sul PCE Core YoY, prevista per il 28 marzo 2026, sarà particolarmente importante per comprendere meglio le tendenze inflazionistiche negli Stati Uniti.

In conclusione, il contesto attuale è caratterizzato da un'elevata incertezza, con gli investitori che devono navigare in un mare di notizie geopolitiche e dati economici. La prudenza e un'attenta valutazione delle opportunità di investimento sono consigliate in questo periodo di alta volatilità.
audio_script_en:
Good morning, and welcome to today's edition of Bloomberg Radio. I'm your host, and we're coming to you live on March 14th, 2026.

The market sentiment is decidedly risk-off today, with a score of 7 out of 10. This is largely due to the escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and their impact on oil production. The VIX index is above 20, the Dollar Index, or DXY, is strong, and the TLT bond is declining. Oil prices have surged above $100 per barrel.

Let's take a look at the key market data. The EUR/USD exchange rate is currently at 1.14, down 0.85% on the day. The Dollar Index, or DXY, is at 100.50, up 0.13%. The S&P 500 is trading at 6632.19, down 0.61%. The VIX index, a measure of market volatility, is at 27.19, down 0.37%. The TLT bond, which tracks the 20-year US Treasury bond, is at 86.54, down 0.49%.

The US 10-year yield is at 4.28%, up 0.28% on the day. Gold is trading at $5061.70 per ounce, up 0.18%. Bitcoin is at $70,691, down 0.39%. The Brent crude oil price is at $98.91, down 4.10%. The STOXX 600 index is at 59.13, down 0.55%. The Nikkei index in Japan is at 53,820, down 1.16%. The Shanghai index in China is at 4095.45, unchanged on the day.

Now, let's take a look at some of the key macroeconomic data. The global M2 liquidity proxy is currently at $22.4 trillion, expanding, and up 4.3% year-over-year in the US.

In terms of US macroeconomic data, the last available CPI year-over-year figure, released on February 1st, was 2.7%, down from 2.8% previously. The core CPI year-over-year figure was also 2.7%, down from 2.9% previously. The non-farm payrolls figure, released on the same date, was -92,000, down from 126,000 previously. The unemployment rate was 4.4%, up from 4.3% previously.

The Fed funds rate is currently at 3.64%, unchanged. The PCE core year-over-year figure is scheduled to be released on March 28th, and the GDP quarter-over-quarter figure is scheduled to be released on April 29th.

In terms of news, there are several key stories that are worth mentioning. The hot January PCE inflation figures are likely to keep the Fed on hold, according to some analysts. Investors have slashed their bets on Fed rate cuts as the Iran war sends petrol prices surging. The Iran war is also likely to further damage fragile inflation psychology.

The economic fallout of the Iran war is a major concern, and the impact on US interest rates is also being closely watched. The Japanese leader is wrestling with the Middle East shock in a planned trip to Washington. Sudan's devastating war rages on as regional rivalries deepen.

In conclusion, the market sentiment is risk-off today due to the escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The VIX index is above 20, and the DXY is strong. Oil prices have surged above $100 per barrel. The macroeconomic data is mixed, but the Fed funds rate is unchanged.

As we move forward, there are several key things to watch. The PCE core year-over-year figure is scheduled to be released on March 28th, and the GDP quarter-over-quarter figure is scheduled to be released on April 29th. The Iran war and its impact on oil prices and inflation will be closely watched. The market will also be watching for any signs of a shift in Fed policy.

In terms of specific assets, the EUR/USD exchange rate is likely to be impacted by the US dollar's strength. The S&P 500 is likely to be impacted by the risk-off sentiment. The gold price is likely to be supported by the risk-off sentiment. The oil price is likely to be impacted by the Iran war.

Overall, it's a complex and uncertain market environment, and investors will need to be closely watching a range of factors in order to make informed decisions.

Looking ahead to tomorrow, investors will be watching for any further developments in the Iran war and its impact on oil prices and inflation. They will also be watching for any signs of a shift in Fed policy and the release of key macroeconomic data. 

The market is likely to remain volatile, and investors will need to be prepared for a range of possible outcomes. We'll be back with more on this and other stories after the break.

articles.title: Military Senior Service Colleges Require Reform, But There Sure Are Some Bad Ideas Out There
articles.snippet: “No one is thinking if everyone is thinking alike.” Gen. George S. Patton’s quip shows that he not only understood the advantage of audacious combat leadership, but the necessity to develop leaders and organizations that think critically and challenge assumptions. Military education is at the forefront of this endeavor in professional militaries. Indeed, last week Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth ordered a review of the military’s senior service/war colleges and the severing of ties between the department and 22 academic institutions in an effort to better prepare strategic leaders for the complexities of modern warfare.In a recent opinion article, Thomas The post Military Senior Service Colleges Require Reform, But There Sure Are Some Bad Ideas Out There appeared first on War on the Rock

articles.title: Trump Bombs Iran’s Kharg Island and Threatens to Hit Oil Exports
articles.snippet: U.S. President Donald Trump said American forces had launched a major bombing raid on Iran’s strategic Kharg Island, targeting military facilities on the key Persian Gulf outpost while warning Tehran that its vital oil infrastructure could be destroyed if shipping in the Strait of Hormuz is disrupted. In a social media statement, Trump said that at his direction the United States Central Command carried out “one of the most powerful bombing raids in the history of the Middle East,” claiming U.S. forces had “totally obliterated…

articles.title: Bank of America: Sell Oil Above $100
articles.snippet: Bank of America is warning investors not to chase oil prices higher if crude climbs above $100 per barrel, arguing that prices at those levels tend to trigger government and central bank responses aimed at cooling the economy. The bank said oil has already been one of the biggest market movers this year. Crude prices are up nearly 70% year-to-date, far outpacing broader commodities, which are up about 41%, and gold, which has gained roughly 17%. Over the same period, the S&P 500 has fallen about 2.5%, while bitcoin has dropped around 20%. According…

articles.title: The World’s Biggest Oil Supply Shock Is Now Unfolding
articles.snippet: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that the current energy crisis triggered by the war in the Middle East marks the “largest disruption” in the history of the oil market. The group made a record intervention in the market on Wednesday, with the release of 400m barrels from the strategic reserves. But the move has made little difference to the surging price of Brent crude – the international benchmark for oil – which once again eclipsed the $100 mark on Thursday on the news two Iraqi tankers had been struck…

articles.title: China’s Sinopec to Slash Refinery Rates amid Crude Supply Shock
articles.snippet: The top Asian refiner, China’s state-controlled Sinopec, plans to slash this month its refinery processing rates by 11-13% as crude supply is choked by the Middle East war, Reuters reported on Friday, quoting sources with knowledge of the refining giant’s operations. Sinopec, whose refineries account for a third of all Chinese throughput, plans to lower its crude runs by between 600,000 and 700,000 barrels per day (bpd) in March, from initial plans to process 5.2 million bpd. The loss in refinery throughput does not include regular…

articles.title: Iran’s All-or-Nothing Game in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.snippet: Brent crude is set to close above $100 for the first time since 2022 as tensions around the Strait of Hormuz intensify. ICE Brent is set to settle above $100 per barrel for the first time since August 2022, undeterred by the US’ easing of sanctions on Russia and the IEA’s mulled release of strategic petroleum stocks. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz sits front and centre to any oil price outlook, with Iran already striking at least 18 vessels since the US-Iran conflict started two weeks ago. Iran’s New Supreme Leader Goes All…
